claudeAnalysis.outcomePrediction: Republicans are favoured to hold the Senate while the House remains highly competitive; historical midterm patterns suggest some Democratic seat gains but full House flip is uncertain.
claudeAnalysis.electionBriefing: The November 2026 midterms will put all 435 House seats and 33 Senate seats on the line — a major referendum on the Trump administration. Democrats are defending several vulnerable Senate seats in Republican-leaning states while trying to claw back the House. With generic ballot polling within 1 point, both chambers are genuinely competitive.
riskFactors: economic recession dampening incumbent party turnout
